title: Add --max-turns and --max-budget-usd cost controls to agent delegation
source: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/sdk - Claude Code CLI flags for cost control and iteration limits
description: delegate_to_agent() (line 673) only uses a timeout for cost control, but an agent can burn significant tokens within that window. Claude Code now supports --max-turns N (limit agentic round-trips) and --max-budget-usd X (hard spend limit per invocation). Adding these prevents runaway agents, especially for complex tickets with Opus models where costs can escalate quickly. The AGENT_MODELS mapping already differentiates model costs — pairing it with turn/budget limits per complexity tier adds real cost governance.
proposed_changes: 1. Add AGENT_LIMITS dict in delegate.py mapping complexity to limits: {'XS': {'max_turns': 10, 'max_budget': 0.50}, 'S': {'max_turns': 20, 'max_budget': 1.00}, 'M': {'max_turns': 30, 'max_budget': 2.00}, 'L': {'max_turns': 50, 'max_budget': 5.00}, 'XL': {'max_turns': 80, 'max_budget': 10.00}}. 2. Update delegate_to_agent() signature to accept max_turns and max_budget_usd params. 3. Add '--max-turns', str(max_turns) and '--max-budget-usd', str(max_budget) to the cmd list when provided. 4. In build_prompt() and cmd_delegate(), look up ticket complexity and pass the corresponding limits. 5. Update meeseeks.py similarly with tight limits: max_turns=15, max_budget=0.50 to enforce the 'quick task' contract. 6. Emit roro events with cost metadata when limits are hit.
